evidence: The statement concerns an open subset of the unit square 'with measure alpha' (volume A), so Lebesgue measure is load-bearing in Carbery's rectangle problem.
The solved loose bound is stated for sets of Lebesgue measure alpha in the unit square (volume A).toReal.
Problem 9(i) asks for r_3(N) << N(log N)^{-10}, solved by Bloom-Sisask 'Breaking the logarithmic barrier in Roth's theorem on arithmetic progressions' [BlSi20].
r_3(N) is defined as the size of the largest subset of {1,...,N} without non-trivial 3-term arithmetic progressions, i.e. the largest Salem-Spencer set.
The quantity r_k(N) counts the largest subset of {1,...,N} without non-trivial k-term arithmetic progressions.
The problem asks whether every measurable A of positive (Lebesgue) volume contains an affine copy of {1, 1/2, 1/4, ...}.
The solved outer-measure variant asks the same for sets of positive Lebesgue outer measure (volume A > 0 without measurability).
The docstring states Hilbert's 17th problem verbatim: every non-negative multivariate polynomial is a sum of squares of rational functions (resolved by Artin, 1927).
Hilbert's characterization of the (n,d) pairs where non-negative homogeneous polynomials are sums of squares of polynomials, the polynomial variant of Hilbert's 17th problem.
The docstring states the Hilbert-Smith conjecture: every locally compact topological group acting continuously and faithfully on a connected finite-dimensional manifold is a Lie group.
Docstring: 'Equivalent p-adic formulation ... By the Gleason-Yamabe theorem, this is equivalent to hilbert_smith_conjecture' - the same conjecture stated via Z_p actions.
Solved special case of the Hilbert-Smith conjecture for isometric actions on Riemannian manifolds via Myers-Steenrod.
Pardon (2013, arXiv:1112.2324) proved the Hilbert-Smith conjecture for 3-dimensional manifolds.
Docstring: 'Hilbert's fifth problem (Gleason-Montgomery-Zippin, 1952): every locally Euclidean topological group is a Lie group.'
The conclusion of both Hilbert's fifth problem and the Hilbert-Smith conjecture is that the group admits a Lie group structure (LieGroup (R k) T G).
The hypothesis equates the sums over group elements of phi(|g|), 'where phi is the Euler totient function'.
Kourovka 19.25 asks: if G is simple and the totient sums agree, is H necessarily simple (IsSimpleGroup G -> IsSimpleGroup H)?
Kourovka 20.76 (Pyber) is about a finite p-group G (IsPGroup p G) whose abelian normal subgroups have order at most p^k.
The bound p^k on abelian normal subgroups and the conclusion p^{2k} on all abelian subgroups make abelian subgroups the core of the statement.
Docstring: 'Eisenstein's theorem (1852): an algebraic power series over Q[z] has bounded denominators', exactly the content of the Wikidata item on coefficients of algebraic power series.
The Lam-Litt conjecture concerns power series solutions of algebraic ODEs (IsSolutionOfAlgebraicODE) with coefficients in Z[1/N] being algebraic.
The 3)=>2) implication of the Lam-Litt conjecture is stated for power series solutions of an algebraic ODE given by a rational function g.
